# synix

> A build system for agent memory — declarative pipelines that turn raw conversations into searchable, hierarchical memory with full provenance tracking

## What it does

Synix transforms conversation exports (ChatGPT, Claude, text files) into structured memory artifacts through configurable pipelines of LLM transforms. Change a prompt or add new sources — only affected layers rebuild. Every artifact traces back through the dependency graph to its source conversations. The fundamental output is system prompt + RAG, built from raw conversations with full lineage tracking.

## Key concepts

- **Artifact** — immutable, versioned build output (transcript, episode, rollup, core memory). Content-addressed via SHA256
- **Layer** — typed Python object in the build DAG. `Source` for inputs, `Transform` subclasses for LLM steps, `SearchSurface`/`SynixSearch`/`FlatFile` for projections
- **Pipeline** — declared in Python. `Pipeline.add(*layers)` routes transforms to layers, projection declarations to manifest
- **Projection** — structured declaration materialized at release time (search.db, context.md). Not built during `synix build`
- **Release** — `synix release HEAD --to <name>` materializes projections from a snapshot into `.synix/releases/<name>/`
- **Provenance** — every artifact traces back to inputs via `parent_labels`. Always included in search results
- **Cache/Rebuild** — fingerprint comparison: if inputs, prompt, or transform source changed, rebuild. Otherwise skip

## Installation and quick start

```bash
# Install with uv
uvx synix init my-project
cd my-project

# Add API key, then build and release
uvx synix build                    # produce immutable snapshot
uvx synix release HEAD --to local  # materialize projections
uvx synix search "query" --release local
```

## CLI commands

```bash
synix build pipeline.py                          # Produce immutable snapshot in .synix/
synix release HEAD --to local                    # Materialize projections to named release
synix search "query" --release local             # Search a release target
synix lineage <artifact-id>                      # Provenance tree
synix list                                       # All artifacts in current snapshot
synix show <id>                                  # Render artifact content
synix plan pipeline.py                           # Dry-run with cost estimates
synix diff <ref-a> <ref-b>                      # Structured diff between refs
synix clean                                      # Remove releases and work state
```

## Architecture overview

Core modules:
- `src/synix/core/models.py` — Layer hierarchy (Source, Transform, SearchSurface, FlatFile, Pipeline)
- `src/synix/build/runner.py` — Execute pipeline, walk DAG, run transforms, cache artifacts
- `src/synix/build/object_store.py` — Content-addressed storage (.synix/objects/)
- `src/synix/build/snapshot_view.py` — Read API over immutable snapshots
- `src/synix/search/` — FTS5 + semantic search with provenance

State model:
```
.synix/
├── objects/           # content-addressed artifacts, manifests, snapshots
├── refs/             # git-like refs (heads/main, runs/*, releases/*)
├── releases/         # materialized projections per release name
└── work/             # build-time transient data
```

## Pipeline definition

Pipelines are Python code with object references for dependencies:

```python
from synix import Pipeline, Source, SynixSearch, SearchSurface, FlatFile
from synix.transforms import MapSynthesis, ReduceSynthesis

pipeline = Pipeline("my-pipeline")
pipeline.llm_config = {"provider": "anthropic", "model": "claude-haiku"}

bios = Source("bios", dir="./sources/bios")
work_styles = MapSynthesis("work_styles", depends_on=[bios], 
                          prompt="Infer work style:\n\n{artifact}")
report = ReduceSynthesis("report", depends_on=[work_styles],
                        prompt="Write team analysis:\n\n{artifacts}")

search_surface = SearchSurface("search", sources=[work_styles, report], modes=["fulltext"])
pipeline.add(bios, work_styles, report, search_surface)
pipeline.add(SynixSearch("search", surface=search_surface))
```

Generic transforms: `MapSynthesis` (1:1), `GroupSynthesis` (N:M), `ReduceSynthesis` (N:1), `FoldSynthesis` (N:1 sequential), `Chunk` (1:N text splitting)

## Important constraints

- SQLite only (no external databases required)
- Local filesystem storage model
- Python 3.11+ required
- Requires LLM API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, or compatible)
- No web UI (CLI and SDK only)
- Single-user focused (no multi-tenancy)
